notes: Every Expected bullet has an explicit instruction: inventory-first gate (SKILL.md:43-45, method.md:14-21), source-miner sweep with cited findings + empty-sweeps-as-findings (SKILL.md:48-50, agents/researchers/source-miner.md:21, method.md:62), guide-before-conversation hard gate (SKILL.md:51-56, method.md:52-53), go/no-go-scoped preliminary analysis (SKILL.md:57-59, method.md:36-39), findings brief with citations + gaps and never-conclude handoff (method.md:85-99, SKILL.md:88-89). No Fails-if triggerable: PRD/conclusions explicitly forbidden (method.md:67-71), analysis ballooning explicitly forbidden (method.md:38-39).

Trigger presupposes a prior analysis ('what we learned from the March tutor study'). The data rule is explicit and sharp: ingesting a prior analysis = synthesis, only new queries on raw data = research (method.md:23-25); SKILL.md when-not section names summarizing a prior study as uno-synthesize's job and workflow step 1 says route-and-stop (SKILL.md:43-45). Boundary table method.md:77 repeats it. Fails-if (re-analyzing already-analyzed material) is directly blocked.

LIVE-EXECUTED the sweep the skill prescribes (explorer dispatch, INDEX.md check, component-discovery.md steps: inventory.md -> stories -> src grep). Correct cited answer reached: NO stacked-avatars component exists; nearest alternatives UserAvatar (single avatar + counter badge, design-system/src/components/UserAvatar/UserAvatar.jsx:16-25, story at design-system/src/specs/Universal/Elements/UserAvatar/UserAvatar.stories.jsx, listed in skills/uno-prototype/references/cheat-components.md:43 and inventory.md:56) and unrelated StackedBarChart. method.md:32-34 mandates honest not-found + nearest alternatives, never naming a nonexistent artifact; explorer.md:21 mandates path:line citations, never file dumps. SCENARIO NIT: Expected says 'answers from cheat-sheet' but the skill's tier-2 load for component discovery is component-discovery.md, whose step 2 starts at inventory.md; the cheat-sheet index reaches the same answer via cheat-components.md, so the bullet is satisfiable either way.

All Expected bullets have explicit instruction: people-scout summoned by the skill (SKILL.md:55-56), Team-DB-ranked candidates with one-line evidence-based why (people-scout.md:20, method.md:46-48), intro drafted / human sends (people-scout.md:10, SKILL.md:56). Fails-if blocked: 'Never contact people' (SKILL.md:87, method.md:49); no-good-match flag mandated rather than stretching (method.md:50, people-scout.md:21). NOTED AMBIGUITY (non-verdict-bearing): SKILL.md:51-56 hard-gates the study guide BEFORE any people-scout dispatch, but method.md:52-53's hard gate only covers guide-before-CONVERSATION; a pure identification ask like this one would, on SKILL.md's literal reading, force a Notion study-guide write before even naming candidates. Both readings satisfy Expected and trigger no Fails-if.

Explicit chain: study-guide writes go through writers/notion (SKILL.md:38-39,52-53); read notion.md before the write, allowlisted surfaces only, never create select options (SKILL.md:53-54); read-only-with-one-exception constraint (SKILL.md:84-85); Research & notes DB is on the allowlist for findings + study guides (docs/conventions/notion.md:18); never-create-options is triple-stated (notion.md:30, SKILL.md:54, AGENTS.md forbidden pattern 16). TWO HARDENING GAPS (prose is unambiguous, so pass): (1) agents/writers/notion.md 'Invoked by' (lines 12-18) omits skills/uno-research - the writer's spec does not acknowledge the study-guide invocation; (2) SKILL.md:16 allowed-tools grants the full mcp__notion-plus__* wildcard (write tools included) to a skill whose prose is read-only-except-via-writers/notion - the guard is instruction-level only, not tool-scope-enforced.

Rubric has ONE hard gate (guide-in-Notion before any SME conversation): enforced verbatim at method.md:52-53 ('Sequencing, not preference'), operationalized as ordered steps in SKILL.md:51-56, and restated in the quality bar (SKILL.md:77-80). Scored dimensions also have enforcing instructions: study-guide-quality (method.md:43-45 - objectives, participant criteria, unbiased protocol, consent), analysis-scoping (method.md:36-39 preliminary/go-no-go rule), sme-precision (method.md:46-50, people-scout.md:20-21). Rubric's applied_by agent exists (agents/reviewers/rubric-applier.md). Rubric benchmark matches golden S1.
